commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer should require the fallback-local fix, current-head proof, and explicit security-boundary acceptance before merge; missing contributor proof makes this unsuitable for automated repair routing.; Needs attention: The diff is focused, but it intentionally changes the approval-runtime authentication boundary and needs maintainer security acceptance before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve fallbac... (sha=9a830a35c1883c075ca0b454d4516f6c395e3cc0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR derives approval-runtime auth from an existing exec-approvals socket token, gates runtime-token attachment to local approval clients, carries requester device identity for remote approval calls, and adds gateway/agent tests.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +315. Total +398 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. From source, current main uses a process-local approval runtime token, and the PR's fallback-local regression is visible by comparing the new target classification with the existing connection-details fallback source.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should require the fallback-local fix, current-head proof, and explicit security-boundary acceptance before merge; missing contributor proof makes this unsuitable for automated repair routing.

target.title: fix(gateway): share approval runtime socket token
target.body:
## Summary
- Use the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token as the approval-runtime token when it exists, so Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate as the local approval runtime.
- Keep the process-local random token as the fallback when no shared socket token exists, without creating `exec-approvals.json` just to read a token.
- Keep `approvalRuntimeToken` attached only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it even when the host is loopback.
- Preserve requester visibility for env-selected approval calls by carrying stable device identity, and keep non-runtime approval clients device-identity capable.

## Scope split
- Split out from #86771.
- This PR is only shared approval-runtime token selection for Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.
- It does not include stale Discord button UX or the `approval.routeNotice.send` gateway method.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A separate approval-runtime process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate with the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token and resolve a requester-bound approval. Env/configured remote gateway URL sources still omit `approvalRuntimeToken` and cannot resolve requester-bound approvals without normal owner visibility/runtime authority. Env-selected approval request/wait calls keep requester identity so two-phase approval flows do not lose their own records after the runtime token is omitted.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `1ef8400457`, Node `v23.11.1`, macOS host. The smoke used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, a real `exec-approvals.json` socket token, a real loopback gateway server with bundled plugins disabled, a parent Node process, a separate child Node process, and real gateway clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` smoke from the repo root with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`. The smoke wrote a temporary `.openclaw/exec-approvals.json`, started a real gateway, created requester-bound approvals, resolved one generated-local approval through `callGatewayTool`, ran a child Node process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` to resolve one approval through `withOperatorApprovalsGatewayClient`, then checked env loopback, env loopback path, and configured remote loopback sources could not resolve requester-bound approvals via local runtime authority.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 ./node_modules/.bin/tsx <pr-87105-current-head-smoke>
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (0 plugins)
[gateway] ready
{"result":"SMOKE pr87105 approval-runtime-boundary ok","head":"1ef8400457","gatewayUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:<port>","sharedTokenFile":"<OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json","localGeneratedResolved":true,"childSharedRuntimeResolved":true,"envLoopbackRejected":true,"envLoopbackPathRejected":true,"configuredRemoteLoopbackRejected":true}
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 13ms
```

Observed result after fix: The generated-local agent gateway path resolved a requester-bound approval with local runtime authority. The second process resolved a requester-bound approval using the shared socket token. Env loopback, env loopback path, and configured remote loopback clients connected to the same gateway but were rejected from resolving requester-bound approvals because those URL sources do not receive `approvalRuntimeToken`.

What was not tested: No Docker Compose smoke was run. The live proof used two Node processes on one host sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME`. Explicit `gatewayUrl` override omission is covered by focused client-option tests because this helper path has no explicit auth input and cannot authenticate to a token-auth gateway as a live remote client.

## Tests and validation
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/gateway.ts src/agents/tools/gateway.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts` — passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/gateway.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — passed; wrapper exited 0.
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/tools/gateway.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 7 files, 83 tests passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean; no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: approval-runtime authentication for local approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.

Mitigation: Shared-token selection only reads an existing socket token; it does not create the shared file. The approval client attaches the runtime token only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it. Env-selected approval calls use stable requester device identity instead of runtime authority, and fail before opening a gateway connection if that identity cannot be loaded.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
